medications: Melatonin 3 mg as needed for sleep
health.mental_health: Early recovery from alcohol addiction; attends support meetings and therapy. Experiences guilt and anxiety about past behavior, sleep disruption during stressful weeks, and uses structured routines to avoid relapse triggers.
recent_visits: Primary care visit for fatigue and lab work follow-up (low ferritin; iron plan adjusted)
Gastroenterology consult for persistent reflux symptoms
Weekly outpatient counseling session focused on relapse prevention and relationship repair

finance.debt: Student loan balance (~$24k) and a small payment plan for a past medical bill from an ER visit related to alcohol use; prioritizing on-time payments to rebuild stability.
investments: Roth IRA (small monthly contributions when possible)
recent_transactions: Rent payment for studio apartment in Midtown Detroit
Co-pay for therapy session and prescription refills (naltrexone, iron)
Monthly bus pass + occasional rideshare after late classes (avoids bars/driving after social events)
Payment to credit card to reduce balance after a prior overspending period during drinking

relationships: Awa Toure (older sister in Dearborn) — supportive but cautious; helps keep him accountable
Jamal Price (best friend from undergrad) — sober ally; meets for coffee and gym sessions instead of bars
Dr. Karen Whitfield (faculty mentor) — professional support; aware he’s in recovery in broad terms
Maya Hernandez (ex-partner) — limited contact; rebuilding trust slowly after past broken promises
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile; posts occasional campus-related updates and photos from Detroit Riverwalk runs. Avoids posting about recovery publicly, uses privacy settings, and rarely tags locations in real time.
private_conversations: Apologies and boundary-setting with family about past drinking episodes
Discussing relapse triggers and accountability plans with a close friend
Coordinating with an ex-partner about whether to attempt reconciliation and what conditions would be needed

schedule.work_hours: Adjunct teaching 2 courses (Tu/Th afternoons) plus office hours (Wed mornings); grading and prep most evenings (10–15 hours/week).
regular_appointments: Support group meeting (Wed 7:00 PM) near New Center
Monthly lab check or follow-up as needed for anemia (varies by semester)
upcoming_events: Midterm exam week preparation and proctoring
Coffee meeting with sister in Dearborn on Saturday afternoon
Department teaching workshop on inclusive pedagogy (next Friday)
schedule.daily_routine: Up early for a quick breakfast and iron supplement, checks class email, commutes by bus to campus, teaches/holds office hours, then grades at home. Evenings are structured around meetings, a short workout, and meal prep to reduce stress and avoid high-risk environments.

professional.company: A public research university in Detroit (teaching appointment; semester-to-semester contract)
projects: Designing a new discussion-based syllabus for an introductory course with more low-stakes assessments
Building a small teaching portfolio site for future full-time applications
Collaborating with a faculty mentor on a conference poster about student engagement in first-year courses
professional.performance_issues: Previously missed a morning meeting last semester due to a relapse-related night; now using strict scheduling, documented lesson plans, and a mentor check-in to prevent repeat issues. Concerned about contract renewal if reliability is questioned.
workplace_relationships: Strong rapport with Dr. Karen Whitfield (mentor) but keeps most recovery details private
Friendly but guarded relationship with department administrator who tracks adjunct attendance and paperwork closely
Positive student feedback, though he avoids informal student social events that involve alcohol

hobbies: Running on the Dequindre Cut and Detroit Riverwalk
Pickup basketball at the campus rec center
Cooking simple West African-inspired meals learned from family
habits: Tracks sobriety milestones privately and avoids bars/house parties where alcohol is central
Meal preps on Sundays to support energy levels with anemia
Uses a sleep routine (screens off by 11 PM when possible)
Keeps non-alcoholic drinks stocked for social situations
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a small studio in Midtown Detroit; chose it for walkability to campus and to reduce exposure to old drinking circles. Keeps a spare key with his sister for emergencies and accountability.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Focuses on iron-rich foods (beans, greens, lean meats when affordable) and pairs with vitamin C; limits coffee around iron dosing and avoids late-night spicy foods due to reflux.
